title: Infosys completes ₹18,000 crore equity share buyback
detailed_summary: Infosys completed its 2025 equity share buyback through the tender offer route. The company bought back 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares at ₹1,800/- per Equity Share for an aggregate consideration of up to 18,000 crore, excluding Transaction Costs. The tendering period ran from November 20, 2025 to November 26, 2025, settlement of accepted bids was completed on December 3, 2025, and the bought-back shares were extinguished on December 4, 2025. The buyback represented 24.31% and 21.68% of the aggregate fully paid-up equity share capital and free reserves on a standalone and consolidated basis, respectively, as at June 30, 2025. Post buyback, issued, subscribed and paid-up share capital decreased to 4,05,46,24,409 equity shares and promoter holding rose from 13.05% to 13.37%.

key_facts.fact: Infosys bought back 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up Equity Shares at ₹1,800/- per Equity Share.
The total amount utilized in the Buyback was18,000 crore (Rupees EighteenThousand Crore only),excluding Transaction Costs.
The Tendering Period for the Buyback commenced on Thursday, November 20,2025 and closed on Wednesday, November 26,2025.
Settlement of accepted valid bids was completed on December 3, 2025, and the Company extinguished 10,00,00,000 equity shares bought back on December 4, 2025.

The buyback represented 24.31% and 21.68% of the aggregate of fully paid-up equity share capital and free reserves as per the latest audited interim condensed financial statements as at June30,2025 on a standalone basis and consolidated basis, respectively.
Valid bids totaled 5,38,090 and total equity shares validly tendered were 82,56,24,353, implying a total response of 825.62%.
Post buyback, issued, subscribed and paid-up share capital was 4,05,46,24,409 Equity Shares of 5/-each, compared with 4,15,44,01,349 Equity Shares of 5/-each pre-buyback.

processing_notes: The document text contains duplicated newspaper versions and OCR/formatting errors. Extracted repeated facts once and used the clearest available values. Post-buyback non-promoter category details appear aggregated or partially blank, so only promoter and total shareholding changes were used.
